proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_009 == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `feature_009 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_022 == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `feature_022 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_024 == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `feature_024 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_014 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `feature_014 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_027 == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `feature_027 == 0`.

analyses.result_summary: Welch test: `feature_009 == 1` mean `dependency_BRAF` was -0.804698 vs -0.249503 in others (n=5,574).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: Welch test: `feature_022 == 1` mean `dependency_EGFR` was -0.601046 vs -0.252407 in others (n=3,880).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: Welch test: `feature_024 == 1` mean `dependency_ERBB2` was -0.685595 vs -0.253473 in others (n=4,890).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: Welch test: `feature_014 == 1` mean `dependency_KRAS` was -0.559415 vs -0.251959 in others (n=11,842).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: Welch test: `feature_027 == 1` mean `dependency_RIT1` was -0.352716 vs -0.257196 in others (n=4,560).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

iterations.update: The first pass found strong marker-like dependencies for all five oncogene outcomes, motivating lineage and adjusted checks.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Skin-lineage cell lines (`feature_011 == skin`) have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than non-skin cell lines.
Brain-lineage cell lines (`feature_011 == brain`) have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than non-brain cell lines.
Breast-lineage cell lines (`feature_011 == breast`) have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than non-breast cell lines.
Pancreatic-lineage cell lines (`feature_011 == pancreatic`) have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than non-pancreatic cell lines.

analyses.result_summary: Skin lines had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.472054 vs -0.293803 in non-skin lines (n=4,935).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: Brain lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.330699 vs -0.275528 in non-brain lines (n=3,565).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: Breast lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.320825 vs -0.291645 in non-breast lines (n=7,007).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: Pancreatic lines had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.426019 vs -0.313272 in non-pancreatic lines (n=5,102).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

iterations.update: Lineage effects were present, but their magnitudes were smaller than the binary marker effects, suggesting marker-driven dependency with lineage enrichment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The BRAF, EGFR, ERBB2, KRAS, and RIT1 marker effects remain negative after adjustment for lineage, subtype, culture, batch, `feature_001`, and all other numeric features.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS for `dependency_BRAF` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `feature_009` of -0.554280.
analyses.method: OLS with one-hot categorical covariates and standardized continuous covariates

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS for `dependency_EGFR` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `feature_022` of -0.349177.
analyses.method: OLS with one-hot categorical covariates and standardized continuous covariates

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS for `dependency_ERBB2` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `feature_024` of -0.432718.
analyses.method: OLS with one-hot categorical covariates and standardized continuous covariates

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS for `dependency_KRAS` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `feature_014` of -0.309702.
analyses.method: OLS with one-hot categorical covariates and standardized continuous covariates

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS for `dependency_RIT1` retained a negative adjusted coefficient for `feature_027` of -0.095469.
analyses.method: OLS with one-hot categorical covariates and standardized continuous covariates

iterations.update: Adjusted models supported the marker hypotheses and left KIF18A and housekeeping-like outcomes for deeper heterogeneity search.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Colorectal-lineage cell lines (`feature_011 == colorectal`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_035 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `feature_035 == 0`.
Higher continuous `feature_028` is associated with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores.

analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.720354 vs -0.252397 in non-colorectal lines (n=5,816).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: `feature_035 == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.544702 vs -0.252583 in `feature_035 == 0` lines (n=9,285).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

analyses.result_summary: A one-standard-deviation increase in `feature_028` was associated with a -0.097271 change in `dependency_KIF18A`.
analyses.method: univariable linear regression with standardized `feature_028`

iterations.update: KIF18A showed stronger heterogeneity than the oncogene outcomes and warranted complete subgroup discovery.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The subgroup `feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_020 == 0`, and `feature_028 >= 1.427` has much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
Within colorectal cell lines with `feature_028 >= 1.427`, `feature_020 == 1` suppresses the KIF18A dependency, making `dependency_KIF18A` less negative than in `feature_020 == 0` lines.

analyses.result_summary: Systematic predicate search found `feature_011 == colorectal` + `feature_020 == 0` + `feature_028 >= 1.427` as the strongest KIF18A subgroup: mean -1.240923 vs -0.267666 in others (n=2,012).
analyses.method: beam search over one-, two-, and three-feature predicates followed by Welch test

analyses.result_summary: Among colorectal lines with `feature_028 >= 1.427`, `feature_020 == 1` lines averaged -0.264228 while `feature_020 == 0` lines averaged -1.240923; the positive effect estimate means loss of dependency when `feature_020 == 1` (n=442 vs 2,012).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test within colorectal high-`feature_028` stratum

analyses.result_summary: Interaction OLS supported the subgroup structure: `colorectal:high_feature_028` coefficient was -0.621017, while `colorectal:feature_020` was +0.457953 and `high_feature_028:feature_020` was +0.300232.
analyses.method: OLS interaction model: dependency_KIF18A ~ colorectal*high028 + colorectal*feature_020 + high028*feature_020

iterations.update: KIF18A was the clearest multi-feature dependency: colorectal and high `feature_028` enabled it, while `feature_020 == 1` largely suppressed it.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher continuous `feature_016` is associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10`, `dependency_DHX9`, and `dependency_POLR2A` scores.
Higher continuous `feature_003` and `feature_018 == 1` identify stronger `dependency_RPL11`.

analyses.result_summary: For `dependency_TMED10`, a one-standard-deviation increase in `feature_016` had slope -0.020328.
analyses.method: univariable linear regression with standardized `feature_016`

analyses.result_summary: For `dependency_DHX9`, a one-standard-deviation increase in `feature_016` had slope -0.021817.
analyses.method: univariable linear regression with standardized `feature_016`

analyses.result_summary: For `dependency_POLR2A`, a one-standard-deviation increase in `feature_016` had slope -0.061230.
analyses.method: univariable linear regression with standardized `feature_016`

analyses.result_summary: For `dependency_RPL11`, a one-standard-deviation increase in `feature_003` had slope -0.054369.
analyses.method: univariable linear regression with standardized `feature_003`

analyses.result_summary: `feature_018 == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.361392 vs -0.252335 in `feature_018 == 0` lines (n=8,055).
analyses.method: Welch two-sample t-test

iterations.update: Several non-oncogene dependencies tracked continuous covariates, especially `feature_016`; subgroup search was needed to determine whether these were concentrated or mostly additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In `dependency_RIT1`, the combination of `feature_027 == 1` and brain lineage (`feature_011 == brain`) is more negative than expected from `feature_027 == 1` alone.
In `dependency_ERBB2`, the combination of `feature_024 == 1`, breast lineage (`feature_011 == breast`), and `feature_013 >= 0.519` has more negative dependency scores than all other lines.
In `dependency_KRAS`, the combination of `feature_014 == 1`, `feature_016 >= 1.088`, and `feature_028 <= -0.406` has more negative dependency scores than all other lines.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_027 == 1` plus brain lineage had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.404687 vs -0.264988 in others (n=329). The `feature_027:brain` interaction coefficient was -0.058229 (p=0.005638).
analyses.method: subgroup Welch test and OLS interaction

analyses.result_summary: `feature_024 == 1`, breast lineage, and `feature_013 >= 0.519` had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.759661 vs -0.293009 in others (n=292). The `feature_024:breast` interaction was modest but significant (coef -0.028089, p=0.029788).
analyses.method: subgroup Welch test and OLS interaction

analyses.result_summary: `feature_014 == 1`, `feature_016 >= 1.088`, and `feature_028 <= -0.406` had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.663974 vs -0.322969 in others (n=265). The `feature_014:low_feature_028` interaction was -0.018254 (p=0.035781).
analyses.method: subgroup Welch test and OLS interaction

iterations.update: RIT1, ERBB2, and KRAS each had plausible modifier subgroups, although the dominant marker remained the main effect for ERBB2 and KRAS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_BRAF`, systematic subgroup discovery will not improve materially over the simple `feature_009 == 1` subgroup.
For `dependency_EGFR`, the complete subgroup `feature_022 == 1`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_003 >= 0.428` has more negative dependency scores than all other lines.
For `dependency_ERBB2`, systematic subgroup discovery identifies `feature_024 == 1`, breast lineage, and `feature_013 >= 0.519` as a concentrated dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_KRAS`, systematic subgroup discovery identifies `feature_014 == 1`, `feature_016 >= 1.088`, and `feature_028 <= -0.406` as a concentrated dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_RIT1`, systematic subgroup discovery identifies `feature_027 == 1` and brain lineage as a concentrated dependency subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Beam search over 120 predicates up to three-way combinations found no useful BRAF refinement beyond `feature_009 == 1`: `feature_009 == 1` alone had effect -0.555195, while the best tiny refinement had effect -0.555412.
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

analyses.result_summary: For EGFR, the search-selected subgroup `feature_022 == 1`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_003 >= 0.428` had mean -0.668717 vs -0.277301 in others (n=276).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

analyses.result_summary: For ERBB2, the search-selected subgroup `feature_024 == 1`, breast lineage, and `feature_013 >= 0.519` had mean -0.759661 vs -0.293009 in others (n=292).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

analyses.result_summary: For KRAS, the search-selected subgroup `feature_014 == 1`, `feature_016 >= 1.088`, and `feature_028 <= -0.406` had mean -0.663974 vs -0.322969 in others (n=265).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

analyses.result_summary: For RIT1, the strongest interpretable search subgroup was `feature_027 == 1` and brain lineage, with mean -0.404687 vs -0.264988 in others (n=329).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

iterations.update: The required heterogeneity search was completed for the five oncogene outcomes; BRAF remained a simple marker effect, while EGFR/ERBB2/KRAS/RIT1 had smaller concentrated subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_KIF18A`, systematic subgroup discovery identifies `feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_020 == 0`, and `feature_028 >= 1.427` as the strongest dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_TMED10`, systematic subgroup discovery identifies `feature_004 == adherent`, `feature_016 >= 1.088`, and `feature_026 >= 1.153` as a concentrated dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_DHX9`, systematic subgroup discovery identifies `feature_015 <= 2.083`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_023 >= 5.669` as a concentrated dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_POLR2A`, systematic subgroup discovery identifies `feature_005 == batch_c`, `feature_016 >= 1.088`, and `feature_018 == 1` as a concentrated dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_RPL11`, systematic subgroup discovery identifies `feature_003 >= 1.003`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_033 >= 0.602` as a concentrated dependency subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: For KIF18A, the search-selected subgroup `feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_020 == 0`, `feature_028 >= 1.427` had mean -1.240923 vs -0.267666 in others (n=2,012).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

analyses.result_summary: For TMED10, the search-selected subgroup `feature_004 == adherent`, `feature_016 >= 1.088`, `feature_026 >= 1.153` had mean -0.357833 vs -0.253216 in others (n=377).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

analyses.result_summary: For DHX9, the search-selected subgroup `feature_015 <= 2.083`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, `feature_023 >= 5.669` had mean -0.437247 vs -0.332983 in others (n=328).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

analyses.result_summary: For POLR2A, the search-selected subgroup `feature_005 == batch_c`, `feature_016 >= 1.088`, `feature_018 == 1` had mean -0.598185 vs -0.418084 in others (n=275).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

analyses.result_summary: For RPL11, the search-selected subgroup `feature_003 >= 1.003`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, `feature_033 >= 0.602` had mean -0.460036 vs -0.268410 in others (n=390).
analyses.method: systematic predicate beam search and Welch test

iterations.update: The required heterogeneity search was completed for all remaining outcomes. KIF18A showed the largest and most interpretable interaction; TMED10/DHX9/POLR2A/RPL11 showed smaller concentrated tails.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_POLR2A`, high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 1.088`) is the parsimonious supported subgroup because extra predicates from the search do not show significant interaction.
For `dependency_RPL11`, high `feature_003` (`feature_003 >= 1.003`) is a broad supported subgroup, with high `feature_016` and high `feature_033` defining a more extreme tail.
For `dependency_TMED10`, joint high `feature_016` and high `feature_026` produces more negative scores than other cell lines, with a negative interaction between those two high-feature indicators.
For `dependency_DHX9`, high `feature_016` and high `feature_023` define a broad negative subgroup, while low `feature_015` marks a smaller, more negative tail.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 1.088` alone had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.528054 vs -0.406961 in others (n=5,002). In the three-predicate model, `high_feature_016:batch_c` and `high_feature_016:feature_018` interactions were not significant (p=0.814 and p=0.375).
analyses.method: Welch test plus OLS interaction check

analyses.result_summary: `feature_003 >= 1.003` alone had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.383051 vs -0.257302 in others (n=5,011); adding `feature_016 >= 0.964` and `feature_033 >= 0.602` marked a tail with effect -0.191626 but weak interaction evidence.
analyses.method: Welch test plus OLS interaction check

analyses.result_summary: Joint high `feature_016 >= 1.088` and high `feature_026 >= 1.153` had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.342437 vs -0.253129 in others (n=490), and the high016:high026 interaction coefficient was -0.036944 (p=0.034841).
analyses.method: Welch test plus OLS interaction check

analyses.result_summary: High `feature_016 >= 0.964` plus high `feature_023 >= 5.669` had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.389195 vs -0.329718 in others (n=3,320); the low-`feature_015` subset was more negative, but interaction terms were not significant in the pairwise model.
analyses.method: Welch test plus OLS interaction check

iterations.update: The final interpretation favors simple dominant markers for BRAF/EGFR/ERBB2/KRAS/RIT1, a clear three-variable KIF18A interaction, and smaller covariate-linked dependency tails for TMED10, DHX9, POLR2A, and RPL11.
